title: Install GTM MarTech Plugin
description: Integrate Google Analytics 4 and Google Tag Manager with your AEM Edge Delivery Services project using a phase-based loading approach optimized for Core Web Vitals.

tasks.title: Create GTM MarTech helper script
tasks.description: Create scripts/gtm-martech.js that imports GtmMartech from ../plugins/gtm-martech/src/index.js, instantiates it with a config object containing placeholder values (empty tags array, empty containers.lazy/containers.delayed arrays, consent: true, analytics: true), includes the DA Preview disable check (const disabled = window.location.search.includes('martech=off')), and exports the instance as default. Include TODO comments where the user must fill in their GA4/GTM IDs and callbacks.

tasks.title: Add preload hints to head.html
tasks.description: Add three lines at the end of head.html: (1) <script nonce="aem" src="/scripts/gtm-martech.js" type="module"></script>, (2) <link rel="preload" as="script" crossorigin="anonymous" href="/plugins/gtm-martech/src/index.js"/>, (3) <link rel="preconnect" href="https://www.googletagmanager.com"/>.

tasks.title: Import GTM MarTech in scripts.js
tasks.description: Add import gtmMartech from './gtm-martech.js'; at the top of scripts/scripts.js with the other import statements.

tasks.title: Wire eager phase into loadEager
tasks.description: In the loadEager function in scripts/scripts.js, add gtmMartech.eager() to the existing Promise.all() call alongside loadSection(). This initializes GA4 scripts during the eager rendering phase.

tasks.title: Wire lazy phase into loadLazy
tasks.description: In the loadLazy function in scripts/scripts.js, add await gtmMartech.lazy(); AFTER loadSections(main) completes. CRITICAL: This must NOT be inside a Promise.all() — it must be awaited independently to ensure correct section/block decoration.

tasks.title: Wire delayed phase into loadDelayed
tasks.description: In the loadDelayed function in scripts/scripts.js, add window.setTimeout(gtmMartech.delayed, 1000); BEFORE the existing delayed.js import. This is only needed if the user configures delayed GTM containers.

tasks.title: Configure GA4 Measurement IDs
tasks.description: Open scripts/gtm-martech.js and replace the empty tags array with your Google Analytics 4 Measurement ID(s) (format: G-XXXXXXXXXX). You can find this in Google Analytics > Admin > Data Streams > your stream > Measurement ID. Multiple IDs can be provided as an array.

tasks.title: Configure GTM Container IDs
tasks.description: Open scripts/gtm-martech.js and add your Google Tag Manager Container ID(s) (format: GTM-XXXXXXX) to the appropriate arrays: containers.lazy for containers that process events (loaded after LCP), containers.delayed for non-critical third-party tags (loaded later).

tasks.title: Configure consent management
tasks.description: If your site requires cookie consent, implement the consentCallback function in scripts/gtm-martech.js. This async function must return an object with Google consent types (ad_storage, ad_user_data, ad_personalization, analytics_storage, functionality_storage, personalization_storage, security_storage) each set to "granted" or "denied". Integrate with your consent management platform (e.g., OneTrust, Cookiebot). If consent is not needed, set consent: false in the config.

tasks.title: Implement event decoration callback
tasks.description: Optionally implement the decorateCallback function in scripts/gtm-martech.js to add custom DataLayer pushes on sections and blocks. This function is called with each section/block element during the lazy phase. Use martech.pushToDataLayer(payload) to send events.

tasks.title: Update ESLint ignore (if applicable)
tasks.description: If the project has an .eslintignore file, add plugins/gtm-martech/* to prevent linting of the plugin source files. If no .eslintignore exists, skip this task.

tasks.title: Review and merge plugin branch
tasks.description: Review all changes on the plugin/gtm-martech branch. Verify: (1) head.html has the three new lines, (2) scripts/gtm-martech.js exists with correct config, (3) scripts/scripts.js has the import and all three phase calls wired correctly, (4) lazy() is NOT in a Promise.all. Create a pull request and merge to main.
